channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Why Singapore’s prime minister gave DBS and Grab a shout-out during the country’s budget speech
item.description: Lawerence Wong called on Singapore's companies to adopt AI “comprehensively”, and described DBS and Grab as firms "moving decisively on AI transformat...

item.title: This crucial state is the latest battleground in redistricting war between Trump and Democrats
item.description: Virginia Democrats fast-track new congressional map that could flip four GOP seats, potentially deciding House control in November elections.

item.title: Lawfare Is the Climate Warriors’ Best Friend
item.description: Some three dozen lawsuits are in the American legal system, with more to come.

item.title: CEOs are still buying into the business case for sustainability, despite Trump’s climate rollbacks
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: Trump’s $12B rare earth plan targets China as experts warn US is ‘one crisis away’
item.description: Trump's $12 billion "Project Vault" aims to break Chinese dominance in rare earth elements, creating America's first critical mineral stockpile for na...

item.title: Why choosing not to hire was the solution for my startup — we raised over $100 million and tripled revenue with the same people
item.description: Our startup, founded in 2020, wanted to understand whether we were building a company to scale or if we were about to mask deeper problems with headco...

item.title: ICE arrests 'worst of the worst' criminal illegal immigrants including murderers and pedophiles
item.description: ICE announces arrests of convicted illegal immigrants, including murderers and drug traffickers from Vietnam, Honduras, Cuba and Mexico, in recent enf...

item.title: Palestinian protester, detained for nearly a year, says 'inhumane' jail conditions prompted seizure
item.description: A Palestinian woman who has been held in an immigration jail for nearly a year after she attended a protest in New York City said she suffered a seizu...

item.title: Workday shed $40 billion in value. Founder Aneel Bhusri is back with a $139 million bet he can turn it around
item.description: Workday's compensation plan for its new CEO underscores how much of an uphill fight SaaS companies have in the face of AI.

item.title: DHS shutdown explained: Who works without pay, what happens to airports and disaster response
item.description: A Department of Homeland Security shutdown is looming after Senate Democrats rejected Republican funding proposals. Here are the employees who will be...

item.title: Texas governor exposes ‘hypocrisy’ of Dem push for states’ rights in Minnesota after Biden years
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott called out the “hypocrisy" of Democrats advocating for states’ rights in Minnesota after he was lambasted for enforcing the bor...

item.title: Sam Altman’s fusion startup Helion Energy hits 150 million degree plasma temperature—a milestone that could bring first grid power in 2028
item.description: Helion Energy achieves plasma at a stunning 150 million degree Celsius, well above the threshold to eventually generate stable fusion power.

item.title: Surrogacy Left These 21 Children Parentless After Alleged Abuse. Until It’s Banned, They Won’t Be The Last
item.description: No amount of regulation can fix a system that is predicated on encroaching on a child’s natural right to his biological mother and father.

item.title: A look at false claims made by the Trump administration as it revokes a key scientific finding
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday revoked the 2009 endangerment finding, which has long been the central basis for U.S. action to regulate greenhouse...

item.title: Arizona GOP candidate drops out of primary, narrowing field to two
item.description: Housing developer Karrin Taylor Robson has suspended her campaign for Arizona governor, saying Thursday she doesn't want a divisive GOP primary that w...

item.title: GOP senator says he's open to compromise on Trump's nominee to chair the Federal Reserve
item.description: Republican Sen. Thom Tillis from North Carolina suggested Thursday he could support a compromise that would allow the Senate Banking Committee to star...

item.title: Scientific studies calculate climate change as health danger, while Trump calls it a 'scam'
item.description: The Trump administration on Thursday revoked a scientific finding that climate change is a danger to public health, an idea that President Donald Trum...

item.title: Democrats Want To Stop Illegal Alien Deportations By Shutting Down DHS. It’s That Simple
item.description: Just like the last time Democrats threw the government into a shutdown, they have no leverage but are now withholding votes to fund the Department of ...

item.title: The ICE Drawdown In Minneapolis Is Probably A Surge To California
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has been rapidly growing its footprint across the country, but California is high on the list of new and expanded ...

item.title: ESPN’s Philly Special Documents The Single Play That United A City
item.description: 'Philly Special' provides new context for die-hard Eagles fans — and fans of comeback, underdog stories.

item.title: Trump Busts Another Taboo
item.description: It used to be that U.S. presidents didn’t pop off on every Tom, Dick, and Harry.

item.title: In Its Latest Act of Repression, Hong Kong Convicted My Father for My Activism
item.description: My father’s conviction is Beijing’s litmus test for Washington: Will the U.S. stand firm in favor of freedom?

item.title: A Congressman’s Five Questions for Munich
item.description: This week’s Munich Security Conference comes at a consequential moment for both U.S. security planning and the future of the Western alliance.

item.title: WV Lawmakers Launch Freedom Caucus To Take On ‘Undocumented Democrats’ In The GOP
item.description: 'It's fitting that we are launching the West Virginia Freedom Caucus on the 250th anniversary [of our nation] to restore those same rights to West Vir...

item.title: Virginia’s Gerrymandering Vandalism
item.description: The Virginia gerrymander would create an extreme deep-blue House caucus in a purple state based upon winning one election cycle.

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
